CHANGE RECORD 
 
When the quality of the products changes, the QuID is updated, and a row is added to this table.   
The third column  specifies which sections or sub -sections have been updated.  The fourth column  
should mention the reason for updating the document . Column 5  should include the names of the 
author(s) doing the changes. Including the name in c olumn 6  shows that the document has been 
validated by the TAC/MFC PQ leader or deputy (or TAC/MFC PQ leader or the deputy). This validation 
means the document was checked for contents, formats and consistency with other documents. 
 
Issue Date § Description of Change Author Validated By

Document adapted to the most 
recent template.

ACRONYM TABLE 
Acronym Description 
AIS Automatic Identification System 
CFAR Constant False Alarm Rate 
CNN Convolutional Neural Networks 
DIDA DMI Iceberg Detection Algorithm 
DMI Danish Meteorological Institute 
DMI-ASIP DMI Automated Sea Ice Products 
EW Extra Wide Swath (Sentinel-1 acquisition mode) 
HH Horizontal polarization transmit, Horizontal polarization receive 
(when considering radar signals)  
HV Horizontal polarization transmit, Vertical polarization receive ( when 
considering radar signals) 
IW Interferometric Wide Swath (Sentinel-1 acquisition mode) 
NetCDF Network Common Data Form 
NRT Near Real Time 
PC Production Centre 
PU Production Unit 
RCM Radarsat Constellation Mission 
RCMLN RCM Low Noise (RCM acquisition mode) 
SAR Synthetic Aperture Radar 
SI TAC (Copernicus Marine Service) Sea Ice Thematic Assembly Centre 
WMO World Meteorolocical Organization 
NRT Near Real Time 
 
KEY DEFINITIONS 
Term Definition 
AIS Automatic Identification System, a maritime safety system that 
ships use to automatically inform about their location, course, and 
other data . Other ships in range (typically 40 -60 nautical miles, 
depending on antenna height and weather) can receive the signals. 
Coastal base stations pick up AIS signals near ports and coastlines. 
Satellites with AIS receivers can capture signals over the open 
ocean, providing near-global coverage. 
SEAICE_ARC_SEAICE_L4_NRT_OBSERVATIONS_011_007 
Ref: 
Date:

September 2025 
1.1 
 
                                                      Page 4/ 19 
CFAR Constant False Alarm Rate (CFAR) detection is a method used in 
Synthetic Aperture Radar (SAR) imagery t o automatically detect 
targets like icebergs, ships, or other objects  against cluttered 
backgrounds such as e.g. the sea surface. Instead of using a fixed 
threshold, CFAR adapts the threshold locally.  
CNN A Convolutional Neural Network is a t ype of machine learning 
model - specifically a  deep learning neural network  - designed to 
analyze grid-like data such as images.
